diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease (CJD) with an R208H mutation of the prion protein gene (PRNP)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was made based on clinical signs typical of sporadic CJD (sCJD), including cognitive decline, gait ataxia, and neurological deficits. The presence of a rare R208H mutation in the PRNP gene, along with neuropathological findings such as spongiform changes, astrocytic gliosis, and an unusual 17-kDa prion protein fragment in Western blot analysis, supported the diagnosis. The case was distinguished from other forms of CJD and prion diseases by the unique combination of tau pathology and the specific mutation.
